case_title: RICARDO PAEZ VS. SEIU LOCAL 790 et al

docket_entries.description: PROOF OF SERVICE OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAEZ, RICARDO
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-22-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021, FORMERLY LOCAL 790'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE TENTATIVE RULING RULE (RE GIVING PROPER NOTICE TO OPPOSING PARTY OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR AT THE HEARING). HOWEVER, THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST SEEKING ADDITIONAL TIME TO FILE THE FURTHER BRIEFS THAT WERE REQUESTED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT AMENDED THE DATES SET FORTH IN THE TENTATIVE RULING AND ADOPTED THE FOLLOWING: THE MOTION IS CONTINUED FOR FURTHER BRIEFING AND HEARING (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS OF BRIEFING). THE PARTIES ARE TO SUBMIT SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON THE SUBJECTS ON NOVEMBER 16, 2007 AND REPLY BRIEFS ON NOVEMBER 26, 207. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM OCT-31-2007 TO DEC-10-2007, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. THE COURT WILL PREPARE A MINUTE ORDER REFLECTING THE AMENDED DATES. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021, FORMERLY LOCAL 790's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.
APPEARANCES: Ricardo Paez, 510/669-0991 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; no other appearances.
RULING: Plaintiff did not comply with the tentative ruling rule (re giving proper notice to opposing party of his intent to appear at the hearing). However, the Court granted plaintiff's request seeking additional time to file the further briefs that were requested in the tentative ruling. The Court amended the dates set forth in the tentative ruling and adopted the following: THE MOTION IS CONTINUED FOR FURTHER BRIEFING AND HEARING. THE PARTIES ARE TO SUBMIT SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS ON THE SUBJECTS DESCRIBED BELOW ON NOVEMBER 16, 2007 AND REPLY BRIEFS ON NOVEMBER 26, 207. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 10, 2007.
1) IS THE PERB DECISION OF AUGUST 10, 2005 A FINAL DECISION OF THAT BODY? IF SO, WHAT BASIS DOES PLAINTIFF HAVE TO BRING THIS CLAIM?
2) THE PERB HAS EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION TO MAKE THE INITIAL DETERMINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. ISN'T PLAINTIFF'S ONLY REMEDY AFTER THAT DECISION, IS A WRIT FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE? HAS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS RUN ON SUCH A WRIT?
The Court will prepare a minute order reflecting the amended dates. (302/PJM/DD-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judicial Notice
